svg.text: InnoDB leaf-page locks: heap_no, gap attachment, and lock_t bitmap
Logical identity (page_id, heap_no); storage: contiguous lock_t header + bitmap in process memory

g.text: Leaf page P: logical order (next-record); hatched intervals are gaps owned by the successor heap_no
Key order is independent of heap_no (id=20 occupies slot 4; id=30 occupies slot 3).
Gap before record R is represented by lock bits on R's heap_no (successor attachment).
Physical heap slots on page P
Slots need not be dense or key-ordered
lock_t bitmap: variable-length object in trx->lock.lock_heap
Bitmap: bit[i] addresses heap_no i on page P
bit[4]=1 denotes heap_no 4 (id=20) on page P
Access: lock_t::bitset() returns Bitset over (const byte*)&this[1], length n_bits/8
Pool alternative: trx->lock.rec_pool[] when sizeof(lock_t)+bitmap <= REC_LOCK_SIZE
Does not block UPDATE of id=20 alone
heap_no indexes the physical record slot on the leaf page. Key order is defined by next-record pointers and need not coincide with heap_no order.
The lock bitmap is the contiguous byte array immediately following the lock_t header in process memory (lock_heap or rec_pool). It is not stored in the tablespace page image.
bit[i] together with rec_lock.page_id denotes (page P, heap_no i). type_mode selects conflict semantics: LOCK_REC_NOT_GAP, LOCK_GAP, LOCK_ORDINARY, or LOCK_INSERT_INTENTION.
The gap preceding record R is not a separate object; it is the GAP or next-key flag on R's heap_no (or on supremum for the final gap of the page).
lock_sys->rec_hash chains lock_t by page_id. Insert conflict examination uses the successor heap_no only.
One lock_t may set multiple bits (locking range scan). Indices beyond n_bits are treated as unset if the page later allocates additional heap slots.
